steps.title: Confirm scene safety
steps.description: Confirm scene is safe. Note child's approximate age and weight (use Broselow tape or age-based estimate if available).

steps.title: Assess airway and breathing
steps.description: Assess: airway patency, respiratory rate (normal varies by age), work of breathing (retractions, nasal flaring, grunting, head bobbing in infants), SpO2, skin colour. Note any drooling, stridor, or barking cough.

steps.title: Position for comfort
steps.description: Position child in position of comfort (usually upright in caregiver's arms for infants/toddlers, sitting for older children). Allow caregiver to stay with child to reduce anxiety.

steps.title: Apply supplemental oxygen
steps.description: Apply O2 via appropriate device for age: nasal cannula (1-2L/min), paediatric non-rebreather, or bag-valve mask with reservoir. Target SpO2 ≥94% (≥92% in known chronic lung disease).

steps.title: Record initial paediatric vitals
steps.description: Record: RR, SpO2, HR, consciousness (AVPU — note: pre-verbal children cannot use 'V'), temperature. Note capillary refill time (normal <2 seconds). Record weight if known.

steps.title: Manage fever (if applicable)
steps.description: If temperature >38.5°C: remove excess clothing, apply tepid sponging, administer paracetamol (15mg/kg) or ibuprofen (10mg/kg) per local protocol if available and not contraindicated.

steps.title: Reassess (every 3-5 min)
steps.description: Repeat vitals every 3-5 minutes (paediatric patients deteriorate faster). Document trend. Alert receiving facility immediately of any deterioration.

steps.title: Determine disposition
steps.description: Record destination facility. Ensure receiving facility notified of: child's age, weight, vital sign trend, work of breathing, any treatments given. Request paediatric standby if available.
